summary: Soundboard recording of decent quality captures a mixed‑quality show highlighted by an outstanding Terrapin > Morning Dew second set and strong first‑set moments.
review: The SBD capture is generally clear with only occasional glitches (Peggy‑O cuts out, some out‑of‑tune notes). Musically the concert swings between a weaker overall vibe – noted by a few reviewers – and high points such as Terrapin > Morning Dew, Peggy‑O, Friend of the Devil and an inspired Estimated Prophet/He's Gone jam. The second set shines while the first set is solid but not flawless.

ai_rating.rationale: Eight detailed reviews give a balanced view: strong musical highlights offset by noted performance flaws and minor recording glitches, resulting in an overall good‑to‑very‑good rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and well‑balanced; a few issues reported such as Peggy‑O cutting out at the solo climax and occasional out‑of‑tune guitar notes, but no major dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is a solid 1977‑style opener with strong tunes but some flubs; the second set delivers a gem in Terrapin > Morning Dew and an adventurous jam sequence, making the concert feel like a high‑energy hometown show.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Beautiful vocal on Friend of the Devil; occasional out‑of‑tune or mis‑played notes (Uncle John's Band) and a coda during He's Gone.
band_member_comments.Drums: Short drum segment (<3 min) but still energetic; mention of Sampson coming out of drums.
